title: Terminal Grep Tool
description: Build a fast command-line utility to search for strings within files, implementing basic regex-like matching and file I/O in Rust.
requirements: Handling CLI arguments and user input.
Error handling with Result and Option.
Iterating over strings and performing searches.
hints: Use `std::env::args()` to get the arguments passed to your program.
The `lines()` method on a string returns an iterator over the lines of the string.
Don't forget to handle the case where the user doesn't provide enough arguments!
expectedOutput: A compiled binary that can be run from the terminal to search for text inside local files.
